title: Fig. 2 in Transcriptional induction of the IMD signaling pathway and associated antibacterial activity in the digestive tract of cat fleas (CtenoCephalideS feliS)
author: Weber, Katie
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Fig. 2 Relative mRNA levels of genes involved in the Toll pathway. The expression of A PGRP-SA, B Toll, and C cactus was measured by qPCR in pooled flea samples (n = 40) from each of the four treatment groups (C control sterile blood, B.h. blood infected with Bartonella henselae, S.m. blood infected with Serratia marcescens, M.l. blood infected with Micrococcus luteus) at two exposure time points (4 and 24 h). Data are shown as the mean (± SEM) relative mRNA levels from 3 independent trials combined. The data were analyzed by ANOVA followed by Dunnett's multiple comparison test in GraphPad Prism V8. Significant differences in relative mRNA levels are depicted as they relate to the control group only. ns, not significant
